Name: New Recruits
Effect: +1 Strength
Flavor: Numbers aren't everything. They're not nothing either.

Name: Sharpened Blades
Effect: +2 Strength
Flavor: Old-world steel, maintained by people who know what they're doing.

Name: Cannons
Effect: +3 Strength
Flavor: Too loud for subtlety. Nobody using these is interested in subtlety.

Name: Navigator
Effect: +1 Movement
Flavor: Knows the corridors, the crossings, the shortcuts nobody mapped.

Name: Landship
Effect: +2 Movement
Flavor: Only the Versari were supposed to have these. Supposed to.

Name: Captain
Effect: +1 Strength, +1 Movement
Flavor: Someone has to know where they're going and why.

Name: Heavy Armor
Effect: +2 Strength, -1 Movement
Flavor: Harder to kill. Easier to outmaneuver. Pick your problem.

Name: Veteran Crew
Effect: +2 Strength, +1 Movement
Flavor: They've done this before. That's the whole advantage.

Name: Field Medics
Effect: After a failed contest this unit is not forced to retreat.
Flavor: Losing ground isn't the same as losing the unit.

Name: Scout Runners
Effect: +1 Movement. This unit may pass through enemy-held locations without triggering a contest.
Flavor: The fastest way through is usually invisible.

Name: Iron Discipline
Effect: This unit ignores the first failed contest result each round.
Flavor: The difference between a rout and a delay.

Name: Saboteurs
Effect: Dual-slot. On a successful contest disable one chip installed at the contested location.
Flavor: They don't win fights. They make sure the other side can't either.

Name: War Banner
Effect: Dual-slot. +2 Strength. Friendly units contesting the same location gain +1 Strength.
Flavor: It doesn't make them braver. It reminds them they're not alone.

Name: Outriders
Effect: +2 Movement, +1 Strength. Dual-slot.
Flavor: They're already there by the time you finish the order.

Name: Recyclers
Effect: +1 Scrap production
Flavor: Nothing leaves. Everything becomes something else.

Name: Factory
Effect: +2 Scrap production
Flavor: The old machines still work if you know how to ask them.

Name: Defense Turrets
Effect: +2 garrison Strength
Flavor: Installed overnight. Effective immediately.

Name: Training Grounds
Effect: +1 to your unit cap. Enables recruiting units at this location.
Flavor: A militia becomes an army one bad season at a time.

Name: Town Hall
Effect: +1 to this location's foothold cap.
Flavor: Legitimacy is a resource too.

Name: Recon Team
Effect: When you draw an encounter you may discard it and draw again.
Flavor: They go first. You decide after.

Name: Logistics Hub
Effect: +1 Action each of your turns while you hold this location.
Flavor: Movement is power. Everything else is waiting.

Name: Labs
Effect: +1 Tech score.
Flavor: Someone was doing research here. Whether it was good research is still being determined.

Name: Watchtower
Effect: Passive: You are informed when any enemy unit moves adjacent to this location.
Flavor: The first warning is worth more than the best response.

Name: Barricades
Effect: Enemy units must spend 1 additional Scrap to initiate a contest here.
Flavor: Not impossible to breach. Just expensive.

Name: Supply Depot
Effect: Friendly units at this location may spend 1 Scrap to gain +2 Strength for one contest.
Flavor: The unglamorous work that decides unglamorous battles.

Name: Salvage Yard
Effect: Gain 1 Scrap when any contest at this location concludes regardless of outcome.
Flavor: Win or lose something useful comes out of a fight.

Name: Broadcast Tower
Effect: You may reroll a single dice roll during one of your contests within 2 hexes of this location. Once per turn.
Flavor: Presence isn't just physical.

Name: Relay Station
Effect: Activated (free, once per turn): Discard one reactive card, then draw one reactive card.
Flavor: Information moves faster than armies. Barely.

Name: Fortified Gate
Effect: Dual-slot. +3 garrison Strength. Enemy units that fail a contest here retreat two hexes.
Flavor: Some doors say everything before anyone speaks.

Name: Munitions Cache
Effect: Activated (5 Scrap): Destroy one chip at an enemy held location within 3 hexes.
Flavor: Sometimes the cleanest move is scorched earth.
